description_long: This house features a steeply pitched gable roof with a reddish-brown color and several small, raised roof tiles for added detail. The exterior walls are light gray with dark brown timber framing, evoking a traditional half-timbered architectural style. The base of the house is a solid blue-gray foundation with a few scattered stone accents. There are no visible windows or doors on any side, giving the structure a simple, almost stylized appearance. The roof overhangs slightly on all sides, providing some protection to the upper walls. The overall design is compact and symmetrical, with clean lines and minimal ornamentation.
description: A compact, half-timbered house with a steep reddish-brown gable roof and a blue-gray stone foundation. The exterior is simple, with no visible windows or doors and clean, symmetrical lines.
description_view_0: The house is seen from the gable end, showing the triangular roof peak, timber framing, and a blue-gray foundation with two stone accents.
description_view_1: This view shows the house from the side, highlighting the broad roof surface with several raised tiles and the timber-framed wall with a more complex pattern.
description_view_2: The opposite gable end is visible, similar to view 0 but with a slightly different timber pattern and two stone accents on the foundation.
description_view_3: The other side view, mirroring view 1, with a broad roof surface, raised tiles, and a simpler timber pattern on the wall.
attribute_entryway_design: no visible entryway; simple facade with no door or porch
